data.title: US stock futures rise ahead of CPI report, with Dow Jones up 0.04%, S&P 500 up 0.23%, Nasdaq 100 up 0.41%. Intel stock jumps 8.44% after beating earnings. Ford Motor rises 2.51% with better-than-expected results. Fed likely to cut rates in October meeting.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose following Thursday's positive movements, with major benchmark indices showing higher futures. While the impending government shutdown caused a delay, the Bureau of Labor Statistics was set to release the September Consumer Price Index report. Trump terminated trade negotiations with Canada over a tariff ad dispute. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.01%, and the Fed is likely to cut interest rates in October. Some key futures changes were: Dow Jones 0.04%, S&P 500 0.23%, Nasdaq 100 0.41%, Russell 2000 0.27%. Stocks such as Intel (INTC) and Ford (F) saw rises in premarket trading after strong financial performances. INTC reported revenue of $13.65 billion, beating estimates, and F had quarterly earnings of 45 cents per share, surpassing analyst expectations. INTC maintained a strong price trend across different terms.

data.title: Top-tier media test drove G700 at Hongyangshan Automotive Sports Park, proving its off-road capability on challenging courses.
data.description: In Wuhu, China, top-tier media from around the world participated in the global debut test drive of the G700 at Anhui’s Hongyangshan Automotive Sports Park as part of the “Innovation · Defining the Future” JETOUR Global Media Alliance Tech Tour 2025. The G700 showcased its Smart Off-Road System, demonstrating effortless control on all terrains for easy escapes. The field tests included challenging courses with mogul sections and steep mounds to test the vehicle's capabilities. The G700 successfully navigated undulating and slippery sections with ease.

data.title: Intel's shares rise 8% in pre-market trading after reporting Q3 revenue of $13.65B, beating estimates. Other stocks moving are QLGN (+106.7%) and WGRX (+74.4%).
data.description: Shares of Intel Corporation (NASDAQ: INTC) surged in pre-market trading following better-than-expected third-quarter financial results. Intel reported $13.65 billion in revenue for the quarter, exceeding analyst estimates of $13.14 billion. They also reported adjusted earnings of 23 cents per share, beating estimates of one cent per share. Intel shares rose 8.4% to $41.37. Other stocks moving in pre-market trading included Qualigen Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ: QLGN), up 106.7% to $7.30 after a partnership announcement with BitGo, and Wellgistics Health, Inc. (NASDAQ: WGRX), up 74.4% to $0.7036 following a 9% gain the previous day and a letter of intent with Datavault AI Inc. (NASDAQ: DVLT). Inhibrx Biosciences, Inc. (NASDAQ: INBX) also surged 54.6% to $43.85 after successful results.

data.title: Bonerge unveils 2nd-gen Amuctive™ Akkermansia with 7.6× higher Amuc_1100, revolutionizing probiotics.
data.description: A new product has been introduced by Bonerge in New York under the name of 2nd-gen Amuctive™ Akkermansia. This product boasts up to 7.6 times higher levels of Amuc_1100 compared to previous versions. This advancement is set to redefine the standards for probiotic potency and measurable efficacy in the industry.
